+ French Numbers +

+

+ + {/* Learning Tips */} +
+

💡 Tips for Learning French Numbers

+
    +
  • Numbers 1-16 are unique and must be memorized
    • +
  • 17-19 follow the pattern "dix" + number (ten + number)
    • +
  • 70 = sixty-ten (soixante-dix), 80 = four-twenties (quatre-vingts)
    • +
  • 90 = four-twenty-ten (quatre-vingt-dix)
    • +
  • Practice with the ranges in Practice mode!
    • +
+

+

+ French Possessive Adjectives +

+

+ Depends on the noun form (masculine/feminine/plural), not the owner. +

+ + + +
+
+

A. My

+
+ {possessiveAdjectivesData.filter((item) => item.french === 'mon' || item.french === 'ma' || item.french === 'mes').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

B. Your

+
+ {possessiveAdjectivesData.filter((item) => item.french === 'ton' || item.french === 'ta' || item.french === 'tes' || item.french === 'votre' || item.french === 'vos').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

C. Our

+
+ {possessiveAdjectivesData.filter((item) => item.french === 'nos').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

D. Their

+
+ {possessiveAdjectivesData.filter((item) => item.french === 'leur' || item.french === 'leurs').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

E. His / Her

+
+ {possessiveAdjectivesData.filter((item) => item.french === 'son' || item.french === 'sa' || item.french === 'ses').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+
+
+ + ) : learnMode === 'disjunctive' ? ( + <> +
+

+ Disjunctive (Stressed) Pronouns +

+

+ Tap any pronoun to hear pronunciation. +

+ + + +
+

First Person

+
+ {disjunctivePronounsData.filter((item) => item.french === 'moi' || item.french === 'nous').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

Second Person

+
+ {disjunctivePronounsData.filter((item) => item.french === 'toi' || item.french === 'vous').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

Third Person

+
+ {disjunctivePronounsData.filter((item) => item.french === 'lui' || item.french === 'elle' || item.french === 'eux' || item.french === 'elles').map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

Subject vs Stressed Forms

+
+ + + + + + + + + {subjectVsStressedRows.map((row) => ( + + + + + ))} + +
SubjectStressed
{row.subject} + +
+
+
+ +
+

Learn: What These Pronouns Do

+ +
+

1️⃣ Emphasize the person

+

Used to stress who.

+
    +
  • C’est moi → It’s me
    • +
  • Ce sont eux → It’s them
    • +
  • C’est elle → It’s her
    • +
+
+ +
+

2️⃣ Used alone as an answer

+

When the verb is understood.

+
    +
  • Qui a raison ? → Who is right?
    • +
  • Moi → Me
    • +
+
+ +
+

3️⃣ Used after prepositions

+

After: avec, pour, sans, chez, entre, contre, etc.

+
    +
  • Je suis avec toi → I am with you
    • +
  • Ces livres sont pour moi → These books are for me
    • +
  • Elle vient chez nous → She comes to our place
    • +
+
+ +
+

4️⃣ Used for emphasis with subject pronoun

+

Optional but common in speech.

+
    +
  • Moi, je comprends. → Me, I understand.
    • +
  • Lui, il travaille beaucoup. → Him, he works a lot.
    • +
+
+
+ +
+

⚠️ Important Rules

+
    +
  • Used only for people
    • +
  • Must match gender and number
    • +
  • Often used in spoken French for clarity and emphasis
    • +
+
+
+ + ) : learnMode === 'aller' ? ( + <> +
+

+ Aller (to go) — Present Tense +

+

+ Tap any form or sentence to hear pronunciation. +

+ + + +
+

Present Forms

+
+ {allerPresentData.map((item, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+
+

Aller + à

+

Used for places and buildings.

+
+ {[ + "Je vais à la maison", + "Je vais à l’école", + "Je vais au magasin", + ].map((line, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

Aller + chez

+

Used for people or someone’s place.

+
+ {[ + "Je vais chez toi", + "Je vais chez lui", + "Je vais chez mon ami", + ].map((line, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+
+ +
+

Examples

+
+
+

1️⃣ I am going to your house

+ + +

Second one sounds more natural.

+
+ +
+

2️⃣ I am going to Aarti’s place

+ +
+ +
+

3️⃣ I am going to his house

+ + +
+ +
+

4️⃣ I am going to my friend’s house

+ +
+
+
+ +
+

Learn: When to Use “chez”

+

Use chez when you mean:

+
    +
  • at someone’s home
    • +
  • at someone’s business
    • +
  • visiting someone
    • +
+
+ {[ + "chez le médecin", + "chez le coiffeur", + "chez mes parents", + ].map((line, index) => ( + + ))} +
+
+ +
+

⚠️ Common Mistake

+
    +
  • ❌ Je vais chez la maison
    • +
  • ✔ Je vais à la maison
    • +
  • ❌ Je vais à lui
    • +
  • ✔ Je vais chez lui
    • +
+
+ +
+

🔥 Quick Memory Rule

+

👉 Place → à

+

👉 Person → chez

+
+
+ + ) : learnMode === 'colors' ? ( + <> +

1️⃣ Colors come after the noun

+
+ + +
+
+ +

2️⃣ Colors agree with gender

+
+ + + + + + + + + {[ + ["vert", "verte"], + ["bleu", "bleue"], + ["noir", "noire"], + ["blanc", "blanche"], + ["brun", "brune"], + ["violet", "violette"], + ].map((row) => ( + + + + + ))} + +
MasculineFeminine
{row[0]}{row[1]}
+
+
+ + +
+
+ +
+

3️⃣ Colors agree with plural

+

Add s for plural.

+
+ + + + +
+
+
+ +
+

⚠️ Colors that DO NOT change

+

These stay the same:

+
    +
  • orange
    • +
  • rose
    • +
  • marron
    • +
  • beige
    • +
+
+ + +
+
+ +
+

🔥 Quick Memory Tip

+

👉 If it ends in -e, usually feminine

+

👉 If noun is plural → add s

+

👉 Some colors never change (orange, rose)

+
+

✔ Notes

+
    +
  • These adjectives normally come after the noun
    • +
  • Used to describe personality, condition, truth, quality
    • +
  • Many change spelling in feminine form
    • +
+
+ +

+

{item.english}

+
+ + +
+
+ ); + })} +
+ +
+

✔ Why These Come Before the Noun

+

Most belong to the BAGS rule:

+
    +
  • Beauty → beau, joli
    • +
  • Age → jeune, vieux, nouveau
    • +
  • Goodness → bon, mauvais, meilleur
    • +
  • Size → grand, petit
    • +
+
+ +
+

Examples

+ + + + + +
